rows.description: Summa totalis omnium Recptorum et Remanentium in Cista
rows.notes: Top-of-page title line. Text is legible though a few letters are abbreviated (Recptorum/Remanentium). There is a faint numeric note at extreme top-right but this line functions as the page heading; I treated it as a header and left amounts blank.
Centered section heading clearly written 'Soluta certa'. No monetary amounts aligned with this heading.
Clear alignment with vertical ruling. Pounds '01', shillings '16', pence '8' are crisply written and well aligned in their columns.

rows.description: Coll. Edv. xi p. duabus annis
rows.notes: Description slightly abbreviated but readable. Numeric trio reads as '00 14 6' with clear digits; column alignment is unambiguous.
Short description; amount clearly '00 00 8' (pence 8 visible). Columns are clear and digits unambiguous.
Description is somewhat contracted (Collegio Omn. Animarum). Numbers read '04 12 4' — pounds '04' and shillings/pence align well; handwriting of description slightly faded which lowers confidence.
College of St John Baptism abbreviated. Numeric '00 08 2' is clear and well aligned in columns.
Reads as 'Coll. Baliel' (Balliol). Amount appears as '00 12 6' — shillings '12' and pence '6' clear; the college name spelling is abbreviated and slightly smudged.
Description faint ('Curie Hoystings' uncertain in one letter). Amount reads '00 01 8' but the leading zeros and small '1' are a bit light; chosen based on column alignment.

Description abbreviations present but legible as 'Procurators Cudl. Southn. merton'. Numeric cluster reads '00 19 0' — the pounds digit is faint and could be 00 or 01; chosen '00' because the leading position is aligned with other '00' lines and the '19' shillings is clear.
Simple description. Numeric '01 14 8' reads clearly; digits crisp and properly positioned in the three columns.
Likely 'Coquo' (cook). Amount '01 10 8' is distinct and well aligned.

Description is abbreviated and partly unclear ('Lotric Ann ... mich'). Numbers appear as '02 00 6' — pounds '02' clear, shillings '00' clear, pence '6' slightly cramped; moderate uncertainty in text and minor smudge on pence digit.

Name reads 'Tonson' (legible). Numeric cluster approximated as '00 13 6' — the middle column '13' is readable but the pence digit is slightly faded and could be 4 or 6; chosen 6 based on column alignment with neighboring rows.
Description 'Bibliothecario' clear. Amount looks like '00 13 4' but the pence figure is lightly written and close to an ink blot; chosen 4 as it best matches the small stroke visible.

Line reads 'Bibliothecario non graduat' but the monetary columns look blank or smudged; I record '00 00 0' with low confidence because the pence area has a small mark that could be a smudge or a faint zero.
Description legible. Numeric trio reads '01 03 0' — pounds and shillings clear; pence column shows a faint '0'.
Description partially abbreviated but recognizable. Amount appears '01 06 8' — numbers well aligned though two digits (6 and 8) are slightly cramped.
Long Latin description readable. Amount '00 15 0' is written clearly; no fraction marks. Slight fade in the pounds zero but column alignment supports '00'.
Description clear. Amount '04 00 0' clearly written; pounds digit thicker than surrounding entries which increases confidence.
Description legible. Amount reads '01 00 0' with clear digits in each column.

rows.description: Curate of Southn Ann.1700. 10th mich.1700. 10th Ann.170? 10th
rows.notes: Long entry with multiple date abbreviations; handwriting is darker here. The monetary entry '30 00 0' is very prominent and clearly three digits for pounds (30) followed by '00 0'.
Short description 'Corne pricy' (likely 'corne price'). Amount '00 02 0' is readable though the '2' shillings digit is small.
Description clear. Numeric reads '00 16 4' — shillings '16' and pence '4' legible and within valid ranges.
Abbreviated place names but readable. Amount '00 07 0' appears reasonably clear though the shillings '07' are somewhat faint.

There are two short items grouped (looks like 'Grines { Bocardo 5s 5d }'). The ledger shows a summed numeric '00 10 0' aligned with this group; reading of textual items is moderately uncertain but the numeric sum is reasonably clear.

Short item referring to 'Castle 5s 5d' — the right-hand numeric column shows '00 10 0' written once for the block; there is ambiguity whether these two bracketed items share the same numeric line or each has a line. I record the numeric as '00 10 0' consistent with the visible alignment.
Single-word entry 'Pauperibus' (poor relief). Amount '01 10 0' visible and well aligned; digits are clear though slightly compressed.
Abbreviated description 'Aquad.' (uncertain exact Latin). Amount appears as '00 02 0' but the line is near a shadow and slightly faded which reduces confidence.
Reads 'Bibliorum Clerico' (clerk of the books). Right-hand numeric '00 02 0' visible but faint; column alignment supports this read.
Final total boxed/underlined at page bottom-right clearly reads '59 09 8'. Alignment with the three money columns is unambiguous and digits are bold.
